title: botrychium virginianum f. laurentianum
intro: Botrychium virginianum f. laurentianum, also known as the Laurentian grape fern or grape fern, is a subspecies of the plant Botrychium virginianum. This rare and endangered plant is a type of fern with a unique appearance, featuring sterile, grape-like fronds. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in herbal remedies for its potential to soothe digestive issues and relieve pain.
